# Public Data Collection Checklist

This checklist is for ethical, public-interest research using publicly visible information. Do not bypass access controls, scrape against platform terms, or target private individuals.

## Before collection

- Define the research question.
- Identify public sources only.
- Record terms of service and robots/policy constraints.
- Decide whether user handles should be anonymized.
- Avoid collecting sensitive personal information.

## What to record

- URL or platform.
- Date/time observed.
- Account/page public metadata.
- Content text or caption excerpt.
- Media reuse indicators.
- Engagement counts at observation time.
- Disclosure labels or absence of disclosure.
- Link targets.
- Similar posts or repeated wording.

## What not to do

- Do not access private groups without permission.
- Do not create fake accounts to infiltrate communities.
- Do not buy fake engagement to test vendors.
- Do not share personal data unnecessarily.
- Do not make accusations without strong evidence.

## Analysis outputs

- Timeline.
- Similarity table.
- Network diagram.
- Source-strength table.
- Red-flag matrix.
- Caveats and alternative explanations.
